generated_comment: Yaar, dekho, practically speaking, ghar ka hisaab-kitaab, kids ki schooling, sab meri wife sambhaalti hai. She's just naturally better at managing all that domestic stuff, more systematic. Aur bahar ka kaam, business ki dealings, bank ke chakkar, woh sab main dekhta hoon. Jo jahan strong hai, wahi sambhaale, it's just efficient for the family, isn't it?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Suresh describes his household's division of labor as 'practical' and 'efficient', attributing his wife's role in domestic management and childcare to her being 'naturally better' and more 'systematic', while he handles external financial tasks. This directly encodes the belief that gendered divisions are rational and natural, which is a key surface manifestation of the F16 frame.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 18 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are sitting with three male friends at your farmhouse on a Saturday evening having drinks after one of your friends just got served divorce papers by his wife. Your friend is telling the group what his wife is demanding in the settlement. You are responding and sharing your views on what women want from marriage and what this particular woman's real intentions were from the beginning.
